path: Problem: Remove a fixed number of leading characters from a variable-length numeric code and return the remaining digits as a number
Action: Use LEN to get total length, subtract the number of leading characters to drop, then extract the remainder with RIGHT; coerce to number via *1 (or VALUE)
Observation: Works when the prefix length to remove is constant (e.g., always drop the first character) while the overall code length varies; RIGHT with LEN-based sizing adapts automatically
